
### Setup and Inputs

```{r setup}

rm(list = ls())
gc()

library("officer")
library("psych")
library("broom.mixed")
library("flextable")
library("gtsummary")
library("flextable")
library("tidyr")
library("lmerTest")
library("moments")
library("here")
library("ggplot2")
library("scales")
library("dplyr")
library("data.table")
library("tidymodels")
library("rsample")
library("finetune")
library("forcats")
library("vip")
library("xgboost")
library("pdp")
library("patchwork")
library("shapviz")
library("mice")
library("mgcv")
library("reshape2")

options(scipen=999)
options(width = 200)
options(DT.options = list(warn = FALSE))

```


```{r inputs}

# Get folder location of the data
folder_location <- here("data", "export")
model_folder <- here("data", "models")
results_folder <- here("data", "results")

# Read in the primary data used for Galvanic II modeling
source_df <- fread(here(folder_location, "ads_us.csv"))
full_df <- fread(here(folder_location, "ads_us_full.csv"))
emotion_df <- fread(here(folder_location, "ads_us_emotions.csv"))

# Preliminary preparation and defining columns for feature engineering
FIXED_COLUMNS <- c(
  "session_id", "session_in", "session_out", "phasic_ads", "ars_ads",
  "ads_comparison", "segment_number", "ad_position", "program_country",
  "age_average", "females_pct"
)
IDENT_COLUMNS <- c("session_id", "session_in", "session_out")

modeling_df <- source_df %>% 
  mutate(females_pct = females_pct / 100)

modeling_full_df <- full_df %>% 
  mutate(females_pct = females_pct / 100)

emotion_full_df <- emotion_df %>% 
  mutate(females_pct = females_pct / 100)

```


```{r gender_check}

ggplot(modeling_full_df, aes(x = avg_male_confidence, y = avg_female_confidence)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, color = "steelblue") +
  labs(
    title = "Average Male % vs Average Female %",
    x = "Average Male %",
    y = "Average Female %"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.01)) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.01)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 7),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    plot.margin = margin(5, 10, 5, 10)
  )

```


```{r distribution_check}

# Examine the histogram for each of the variable
vars <- c(
  "ars_ads", "phasic_ads", "avg_intonation_score", "avg_speaking_rate", 
  "avg_loudness", "avg_male_confidence", "avg_estimated_age",
  "neutrality_pct", "happiness_pct",
  "Clout_EN", "Authentic_EN", "Drives_EN", "Cognition_EN",
  "Affect_EN", "Social_EN", "Culture_EN", "Lifestyle_EN", "Physical_EN",
  "Perception_EN", "Conversation_EN"
)

for (var in vars) {
  p <- ggplot(modeling_full_df, aes(x = .data[[var]])) +
    geom_histogram(fill = "steel blue") +
    xlab(var) +
    ylab("Count") +
    ggtitle(paste("Histogram of", var)) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) +
    scale_y_continuous(label = comma)
  
  print(p)
}

summary(modeling_full_df %>% select(vars))

```


```{r data_modification}

# Create a dataset with subset of variables
ars_df <- modeling_full_df %>% 
  select(
    "session_id", "session_in", "session_out", "ars_ads",
    "avg_intonation_score", "avg_speaking_rate", "avg_loudness",
    "avg_male_confidence", "avg_estimated_age", "neutrality_pct", "happiness_pct",
    "Clout_EN", "Authentic_EN", "Drives_EN", "Cognition_EN",
    "Affect_EN", "Social_EN", "Culture_EN", "Lifestyle_EN", "Physical_EN",
    "Perception_EN", "Conversation_EN"
  ) %>% 
  mutate(across(c("neutrality_pct", "happiness_pct"), ~ .x / 100))

# Impute the missing values from average intonation and average speaking rate using MICE
winsorize_vars <- c("avg_speaking_rate", "avg_loudness", "avg_intonation_score", "avg_estimated_age")
imputed <- mice(ars_df, m = 1, method = "pmm", seed = 3825)
ars_df <- complete(imputed) %>% 
  mutate(
    across(
      all_of(winsorize_vars),
      ~ case_when(
        .x > quantile(.x, 0.995, na.rm = TRUE) ~ quantile(.x, 0.995, na.rm = TRUE),
        .x < quantile(.x, 0.005, na.rm = TRUE) ~ quantile(.x, 0.005, na.rm = TRUE),
        TRUE ~ .x
      )
    )
  )

# Perform data transformation before modeling
winsorize_vars <- c(
  "Physical_EN", "Lifestyle_EN", "Drives_EN", "Cognition_EN", "Affect_EN",
  "Social_EN", "Culture_EN"
)
ars_df <- ars_df %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      all_of(winsorize_vars),
      ~ case_when(
        .x > quantile(.x, 0.995, na.rm = TRUE) ~ quantile(.x, 0.995, na.rm = TRUE),
        .x < quantile(.x, 0.005, na.rm = TRUE) ~ quantile(.x, 0.005, na.rm = TRUE),
        TRUE ~ .x
      )
    )
  ) %>% 
  select(-Conversation_EN, -Culture_EN)

# Recheck the distribution for each variable
vars <- c(
  "ars_ads", "avg_intonation_score", "avg_speaking_rate", 
  "avg_loudness", "avg_male_confidence", "avg_estimated_age",
  "neutrality_pct", "happiness_pct",
  "Authentic_EN", "Drives_EN", "Cognition_EN", "Clout_EN",
  "Affect_EN", "Social_EN", "Lifestyle_EN", "Physical_EN",
  "Perception_EN"
)

for (var in vars) {
  p <- ggplot(ars_df, aes(x = .data[[var]])) +
    geom_histogram(fill = "steel blue") +
    xlab(var) +
    ylab("Count") +
    ggtitle(paste("Histogram of", var)) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) +
    scale_y_continuous(label = comma)
  
  print(p)
}

# Create a dataset with the modified data for phasic modeling
phasic_df <- ars_df %>% 
  left_join(
    modeling_full_df %>% select("session_id", "session_in", "session_out", "phasic_ads"),
    by = c("session_id", "session_in", "session_out")
  ) %>% 
  select(-ars_ads) %>% 
  relocate(phasic_ads, .before = "avg_intonation_score")

```


### Helper Functions

```{r bayes_tuning_function}

# Create a helper function to save tuned XGBoost models
xgb_tuning <- function(workflow, resamples, param_info, cache_path, seed = 3825) {
  
  # Create the directory if needed
  cache_dir <- dirname(cache_path)
  if (!dir.exists(cache_dir)) {
    dir.create(cache_dir, recursive = TRUE)
    message("Created folder: ", cache_dir)
  }
  
  # Check cache
  if (file.exists(cache_path)) {
    message("Loading cached model from: ", cache_path)
    return(readRDS(cache_path))
  }
  
  # Run tuning
  message("Running Bayesian tuning...")
  set.seed(seed)
  tuned_result <- tune_bayes(
    workflow,
    resamples = resamples,
    initial = 15,
    iter = 30,
    param_info = param_info,
    metrics = metric_set(rmse, rsq),
    control = control_bayes(verbose = TRUE, no_improve = 10)
  )
  
  # Save and return
  saveRDS(tuned_result, cache_path)
  message("Saved tuned model to: ", cache_path)
  return(tuned_result)
  
}

```


```{r pdp_function}

generate_pdp_plots <- function(
    fit, baked_data,
    target_col,
    features,
    color = "#4A148C",
    title = "Marginal Effect Plots",
    subtitle = "Effect of Each Feature on Residualized Interest Intensity"
) {
  pdp_plots <- lapply(features, function(feat) {
    pdp::partial(
      fit$fit,
      pred.var = feat,
      train = as.data.frame(baked_data %>% select(-all_of(target_col))),
      type = "regression",
      plot = FALSE
    )
  })
  
  plot_list <- lapply(seq_along(features), function(i) {
    ggplot() +
      geom_line(
        data = pdp_plots[[i]], 
        aes(x = .data[[features[i]]], y = yhat), 
        color = color, linewidth = 1
      ) +
      geom_rug(
        data = baked_data, 
        aes(x = .data[[features[i]]]),
        alpha = 0.1, sides = "b"
      ) +
      labs(title = features[i], x = NULL, y = "Marginal Effect") +
      scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.0001)) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(
        axis.text = element_text(size = 7),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        plot.margin = margin(5, 10, 5, 10),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "grey50", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
      )
  })
  
  wrap_plots(plot_list, ncol = 3) +
    plot_annotation(
      title = title,
      subtitle = subtitle,
      theme = theme(
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 5)),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 10))
      )
    )
}

```


```{r interaction_matrix}

get_interaction_table <- function(shap_int, prepped_data, top_n = 15) {
  int_matrix <- shap_int$S_inter
  n_features <- ncol(prepped_data)
  feature_names <- colnames(prepped_data)
  interaction_strength <- matrix(0, n_features, n_features)
  
  for (i in 1:n_features) {
    for (j in 1:n_features) {
      interaction_strength[i, j] <- mean(abs(int_matrix[, i, j]))
    }
  }
  
  rownames(interaction_strength) <- feature_names
  colnames(interaction_strength) <- feature_names
  
  int_df <- melt(interaction_strength) %>%
    mutate(Var1 = as.character(Var1), Var2 = as.character(Var2)) %>%
    filter(Var1 < Var2) %>%
    arrange(desc(value)) %>%
    head(top_n)
  
  return(int_df)
}

```


```{r dependence_plot}

plot_shap_dependence <- function(
    shap_obj, var,
    color_var, 
    title = NULL,
    x_label = NULL,
    color_label = NULL,
    palette = "plasma"
) {
  
  if (is.null(title)) title <- paste0("SHAP: ", var, " × ", color_var)
  if (is.null(x_label)) x_label <- var
  if (is.null(color_label)) color_label <- color_var
  
  sv_dependence(shap_obj, v = var, color_var = color_var) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.position = "right"
    ) +
    labs(
      title = title,
      x = x_label,
      y = "SHAP Value",
      color = color_label
    ) +
    scale_color_viridis_c(option = palette)
}

```


```{r pdp_interactions}

plot_pdp_interaction <- function(
    fit, baked_data, target_col,
    var1, var2,
    title = NULL,
    subtitle = NULL,
    x_label = NULL,
    y_label = NULL,
    resolution = 50
) {
  
  if (is.null(title)) title <- paste0(var1, " × ", var2)
  if (is.null(subtitle)) subtitle <- "Partial Dependence Interaction"
  if (is.null(x_label)) x_label <- var1
  if (is.null(y_label)) y_label <- var2
  
  pd <- pdp::partial(
    fit$fit,
    pred.var = c(var1, var2),
    train = as.data.frame(baked_data %>% select(-all_of(target_col))),
    grid.resolution = resolution
  )
  
  ggplot(pd, aes(x = .data[[var1]], y = .data[[var2]], fill = yhat)) +
    geom_tile() +
    scale_fill_gradient2(
      low = "#FF8A65",
      mid = "#F7F7F7",
      high = "#4A148C",
      midpoint = median(pd$yhat),
      name = "Partial\nDependence"
    ) +
    labs(
      title = title,
      subtitle = subtitle,
      x = x_label,
      y = y_label
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 5)),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 10)),
      panel.border = element_rect(color = "grey50", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5),
      panel.grid = element_blank()
    )
}

```


```{r faceted_plots}

plot_interaction_lines <- function(
    fit, baked_data, target_col, var1, var2,
    title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, n_groups = 3
) {
  if (is.null(title)) title <- paste0(var1, " × ", var2)
  if (is.null(subtitle)) subtitle <- "Cross-Modal Interaction Effect on Residualized EDA"
  
  breaks <- unique(quantile(baked_data[[var2]], 
                            probs = seq(0, 1, length.out = n_groups + 1), 
                            na.rm = TRUE))
  
  if (length(breaks) < n_groups + 1) {
    baked_data <- baked_data %>%
      mutate(group = cut(.data[[var2]], breaks = n_groups, labels = c("Low", "Medium", "High")))
  } else {
    baked_data <- baked_data %>%
      mutate(
        group = cut(
          .data[[var2]],
          breaks = breaks,
          include.lowest = TRUE,
          labels = c("Low", "Medium", "High")[1:(length(breaks) - 1)]
        )
      )
  }
  
  plot_data <- map_dfr(levels(baked_data$group), function(g) {
    subset_data <- baked_data %>% filter(group == g)
    pd <- pdp::partial(
      fit$fit,
      pred.var = var1,
      train = as.data.frame(subset_data %>% select(-all_of(target_col), -group)),
      type = "regression",
      plot = FALSE
    )
    pd$group <- g
    pd
  })
  
  ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = .data[[var1]], y = yhat, color = group)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("Low" = "#FF8A65", "Medium" = "#B39DDB", "High" = "#4A148C"),
      name = var2
    ) +
    labs(
      title = title,
      subtitle = subtitle,
      x = var1,
      y = "Marginal Effect on Arousal"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 5)),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 10)),
      panel.border = element_rect(color = "grey50", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
}

```


### Residual Modeling - Intercept + XGBoost

```{r control_models}

# Fit the control models using the LMER and extract residuals
random_df <- modeling_full_df %>% 
  select(
    session_id, session_in, session_out, phasic_ads, ars_ads, segment_number,
    ad_position, program_format, age_average, females_pct
  ) %>% 
  mutate(program_format = fct_relevel(program_format, "Sports", "Scripted", "Unscripted"))

control_ars <- lmer(
  ars_ads ~ segment_number + ad_position + program_format + 
    age_average + females_pct + (1 | session_id),
  data = random_df
)

# Add the residuals back into the dataset
random_df <- random_df %>% 
  mutate("ars_resid" = residuals(control_ars)) %>% 
  select("session_id", "session_in", "session_out", "ars_resid")

ars_modeling <- ars_df %>% 
  left_join(random_df, by = c("session_id", "session_in", "session_out")) %>% 
  select(-c("session_id", "session_in", "session_out"))

summary(control_ars)

# Fixed effects table
tbl <- tbl_regression(control_ars, estimate_fun = ~ style_sigfig(., digits = 6))

# Extract random effects info
re <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(control_ars))
random_effects_note <- paste0(
  "Random Effects: Session intercept variance = ", 
  round(re$vcov[1], 6),
  ", Residual variance = ", 
  round(re$vcov[2], 6)
)

# Add as footnote
tbl <- tbl %>%
  modify_footnote(everything() ~ random_effects_note)

# Save the modeling results into a Word Doc
tbl %>%
  as_flex_table() %>%
  save_as_docx(path = here(results_folder, "lmer_results.docx"))

```


```{r model_prep}

# Create recipe for trainer
ars_resid_recipe <- recipe(ars_resid ~ ., data = ars_modeling %>% select(-ars_ads)) %>% 
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors(), one_hot = TRUE)

# Create model spec for both trainers
xgb_spec_resid <- boost_tree(
  trees = tune(),
  tree_depth = tune(),
  min_n = tune(),
  learn_rate = tune(),
  loss_reduction = tune(),
  sample_size = tune()
) %>% 
  set_engine("xgboost") %>% 
  set_mode("regression")

xgb_params_resid <- xgb_spec_resid %>% 
  extract_parameter_set_dials() %>% 
  update(
    trees = trees(range = c(100, 1000)),
    tree_depth = tree_depth(range = c(3, 10)),
    min_n = min_n(range = c(2, 20)),            
    learn_rate = learn_rate(range = c(-3, -1)),
    loss_reduction = loss_reduction(range = c(-7, -2)),
    sample_size = sample_prop(range = c(0.5, 1.0))
  )

# Create the workflow used for both trainers
ars_workflow_resid <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(ars_resid_recipe) %>% add_model(xgb_spec_resid)

# Create cross-validation fold for both trainers
set.seed(6297)
ars_cv_resid <- vfold_cv(ars_modeling, v = 10, strata = ars_resid)

```


```{r full_model_tuning}

# Tune models and save as cache
ars_tuned_resid <- xgb_tuning(
  ars_workflow,
  ars_cv,
  xgb_params,
  here(model_folder, "ars_icoria_residuals.rds")
)

# Select best parameters and create workflow
ars_params_resid <- select_best(ars_tuned_resid, metric = "rmse")
ars_selected_resid <- finalize_workflow(ars_workflow_resid, ars_params_resid)

# Fit the final models
xgb_ars_resid <- fit(ars_selected_resid, data = ars_modeling %>% select(-ars_ads))

```


```{r variables_importance}

# Extract variable importance out into a table
ars_importance_resid <- xgb_ars_resid %>% extract_fit_parsnip() %>% vi() %>% arrange(desc(Importance))

# Plot out the variable importance graphs
ars_importance_resid %>%
  head(20) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Variable, Importance), y = Importance)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Ars Ads Variables Importance",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Importance (Gain)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```


```{r partial_dependence}

# Extract the fitted model from workflows
ars_fit_resid <- extract_fit_parsnip(xgb_ars_resid)

# Prep training data through the recipe
ars_baked_resid <- ars_resid_recipe %>% prep() %>% bake(new_data = ars_modeling %>% select(-ars_ads))

# Phasic PDP and ARS PDP
features_voice <- c(
  "avg_speaking_rate", "neutrality_pct", "avg_intonation_score",
  "avg_loudness", "happiness_pct", "avg_estimated_age", "avg_male_confidence"
)
features_liwc <- c(
  "Perception_EN", "Physical_EN", "Affect_EN", "Social_EN",
  "Lifestyle_EN", "Authentic_EN", "Drives_EN", "Clout_EN", "Cognition_EN"
)
generate_pdp_plots(ars_fit_resid, ars_baked_resid, "ars_resid", features_voice, "steelblue")
generate_pdp_plots(ars_fit_resid, ars_baked_resid, "ars_resid", features_liwc, "steelblue")

```


```{r shap_summary}

# Extract the xgboost engine
xgb_engine_resid <- xgb_ars_resid %>% extract_fit_engine()

# Prep and bake using the exact same data the recipe was built on
ars_prepped_resid <- prep(ars_resid_recipe, training = ars_modeling %>% select(-ars_ads))
X <- bake(ars_prepped_resid, new_data = ars_modeling %>% select(-ars_ads)) %>%
  select(-ars_resid) %>%
  as.matrix()

# Create shapviz object
shap_obj_resid <- shapviz(xgb_engine_resid, X_pred = X)

# Summary beeswarm plot
sv_importance(shap_obj_resid, kind = "beeswarm") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "SHAP Summary Plot - ARS Residual Model",
    x = "SHAP Value (Impact on Arousal)",
    y = NULL
  )

```


```{r interaction_analysis}

# Extract interaction table
xgb_ars_engine <- extract_fit_engine(xgb_ars_resid)
ars_prepped <- prep(ars_resid_recipe) %>% bake(new_data = NULL) %>% select(-ars_resid) %>% as.matrix()
shap_ars <- shapviz(xgb_ars_engine, X_pred = ars_prepped)
shap_interact <- shapviz(xgb_ars_engine, X_pred = ars_prepped, interaction = TRUE)

# Create the pairwise interaction table
int_table <- get_interaction_table(shap_interact, ars_prepped, top_n = 30)

# Create cross-model interaction table
voice_vars <- c(
  "avg_speaking_rate", "avg_intonation_score", "avg_loudness",
  "avg_male_confidence", "avg_estimated_age", "neutrality_pct", 
  "happiness_pct"
)
liwc_vars <- c(
  "Clout_EN", "Authentic_EN", "Drives_EN", "Cognition_EN",
  "Affect_EN", "Social_EN", "Lifestyle_EN", "Physical_EN", 
  "Perception_EN"
)

cross_modal <- int_table %>%
  filter(
    (Var1 %in% voice_vars & Var2 %in% liwc_vars) |
    (Var1 %in% liwc_vars & Var2 %in% voice_vars)
  )
print(cross_modal)

```


```{r happiness_interactions}

# Plot dependence plot - Happiness
plot_shap_dependence(shap_ars, "Authentic_EN", "happiness_pct")
plot_pdp_interaction(
  ars_fit_resid, ars_baked_resid, "ars_resid",
  "Authentic_EN", "happiness_pct",
  title = "Residual Model: Authenticity × Happiness",
  resolution = 50
)
sv_dependence(
  shap_interact, v = "Authentic_EN",
  color_var = "happiness_pct",interactions = TRUE
) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "SHAP Interaction Effect: Athenticity × Happiness",
    x = "Authentic_EN",
    y = "SHAP Interaction Value"
  )

plot_shap_dependence(shap_ars, "Affect_EN", "happiness_pct")
plot_pdp_interaction(
  ars_fit_resid, ars_baked_resid, "ars_resid",
  "Affect_EN", "happiness_pct",
  title = "Residual Model: Affect × Happiness",
  resolution = 50
)
sv_dependence(
  shap_interact, v = "Affect_EN", 
  color_var = "happiness_pct", interactions = TRUE
) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "SHAP Interaction Effect: Affect × Happiness",
    x = "Affect_EN",
    y = "SHAP Interaction Value"
  )





plot_shap_dependence(shap_ars, "neutrality_pct", "Physical_EN")
plot_pdp_interaction(
  ars_fit_resid, ars_baked_resid, "ars_resid",
  "neutrality_pct", "Physical_EN",
  title = "Residual Model: Neu × Phy",
  resolution = 50
)
sv_dependence(
  shap_interact, v = "Affect_EN", 
  color_var = "happiness_pct", interactions = TRUE
) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "SHAP Interaction Effect: Affect × Happiness",
    x = "Affect_EN",
    y = "SHAP Interaction Value"
  )

```


```{r intonation_interactions}

# Plot dependence plot - Intonation
plot_shap_dependence(shap_ars, "Authentic_EN", "avg_intonation_score")
plot_pdp_interaction(
  ars_fit_resid, ars_baked_resid, "ars_resid",
  "Authentic_EN", "avg_intonation_score",
  title = "Residual Model: Authenticity × Intonation",
  resolution = 50
)
sv_dependence(
  shap_interact, v = "Authentic_EN",
  color_var = "avg_intonation_score",interactions = TRUE
) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "SHAP Interaction Effect: Athenticity × Intonation",
    x = "Authentic_EN",
    y = "SHAP Interaction Value"
  )

plot_shap_dependence(shap_ars, "Affect_EN", "avg_intonation_score")
plot_pdp_interaction(
  ars_fit_resid, ars_baked_resid, "ars_resid",
  "Affect_EN", "avg_intonation_score",
  title = "Residual Model: Affect × Intonation",
  resolution = 50
)
sv_dependence(
  shap_interact, v = "Affect_EN", 
  color_var = "avg_intonation_score", interactions = TRUE
) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "SHAP Interaction Effect: Affect × Intonation",
    x = "Affect_EN",
    y = "SHAP Interaction Value"
  )

plot_shap_dependence(shap_ars, "Perception_EN", "avg_intonation_score")
plot_pdp_interaction(
  ars_fit_resid, ars_baked_resid, "ars_resid",
  "Perception_EN", "avg_intonation_score",
  title = "Residual Model: Perception × Intonation",
  resolution = 50
)
sv_dependence(
  shap_interact, v = "Perception_EN", 
  color_var = "avg_intonation_score", interactions = TRUE
) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "SHAP Interaction Effect: Perception × Intonation",
    x = "Perception_EN",
    y = "SHAP Interaction Value"
  )

plot_shap_dependence(shap_ars, "Cognition_EN", "avg_intonation_score")
plot_pdp_interaction(
  ars_fit_resid, ars_baked_resid, "ars_resid",
  "Cognition_EN", "avg_intonation_score",
  title = "Residual Model: Cognition × Intonation",
  resolution = 50
)
sv_dependence(
  shap_interact, v = "Cognition_EN", 
  color_var = "avg_intonation_score", interactions = TRUE
) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "SHAP Interaction Effect: Perception × Intonation",
    x = "Cognition_EN",
    y = "SHAP Interaction Value"
  )

```


### ARS Modeling - XGBoost

```{r model_prep}

# Create recipe for trainer
ars_source_recipe <- recipe(ars_ads ~ ., data = ars_modeling %>% select(-ars_resid)) %>% 
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors(), one_hot = TRUE)

# Create model spec for both trainers
xgb_spec_source <- boost_tree(
  trees = tune(),
  tree_depth = tune(),
  min_n = tune(),
  learn_rate = tune(),
  loss_reduction = tune(),
  sample_size = tune()
) %>% 
  set_engine("xgboost") %>% 
  set_mode("regression")

xgb_params_source <- xgb_spec_source %>% 
  extract_parameter_set_dials() %>% 
  update(
    trees = trees(range = c(100, 1000)),
    tree_depth = tree_depth(range = c(3, 10)),
    min_n = min_n(range = c(2, 20)),            
    learn_rate = learn_rate(range = c(-3, -1)),
    loss_reduction = loss_reduction(range = c(-7, -2)),
    sample_size = sample_prop(range = c(0.5, 1.0))
  )

# Create the workflow used for both trainers
ars_workflow_source <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(ars_source_recipe) %>% add_model(xgb_spec_source)

# Create cross-validation fold for both trainers
set.seed(6297)
ars_cv_source <- vfold_cv(ars_modeling, v = 10, strata = ars_ads)

```


```{r model_tuning}

# Tune models and save as cache
ars_tuned_source <- xgb_tuning(
  ars_workflow_source,
  ars_cv_source,
  xgb_params_source,
  here(model_folder, "ars_icoria_source.rds")
)

# Select best parameters and create workflow
ars_params_source <- select_best(ars_tuned_source, metric = "rmse")
ars_selected_source <- finalize_workflow(ars_workflow_source, ars_params_source)

# Fit the final models
xgb_ars_source <- fit(ars_selected_source, data = ars_modeling %>% select(-ars_resid))

```


```{r variable_importance}

# Extract variable importance out into a table
ars_importance_source <- xgb_ars_source %>% extract_fit_parsnip() %>% vi() %>% arrange(desc(Importance))

# Plot out the variable importance graphs
ars_importance_source %>%
  head(20) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Variable, Importance), y = Importance)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Ars Ads Variables Importance",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Importance (Gain)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```


```{r partial_dependence}

# Extract the fitted model from workflows
ars_fit_source <- extract_fit_parsnip(xgb_ars_source)

# Prep training data through the recipe
ars_baked_source <- ars_source_recipe %>% prep() %>% bake(new_data = ars_modeling %>% select(-ars_resid))

# Phasic PDP and ARS PDP
features_voice <- c(
  "avg_speaking_rate", "neutrality_pct", "avg_intonation_score",
  "avg_loudness", "happiness_pct", "avg_estimated_age", "avg_male_confidence"
)
features_liwc <- c(
  "Perception_EN", "Physical_EN", "Affect_EN", "Social_EN",
  "Lifestyle_EN", "Authentic_EN", "Drives_EN", "Clout_EN", "Cognition_EN"
)
generate_pdp_plots(ars_fit_source, ars_baked_source, "ars_ads", features_voice, "steelblue")
generate_pdp_plots(ars_fit_source, ars_baked_source, "ars_ads", features_liwc, "steelblue")

```


### Analysis Views & Comparisons

```{r pdp_compare}

# Compre the partial dependence plots for residual model and model using source ARS
generate_pdp_plots(
  ars_fit_resid, ars_baked_resid, "ars_resid", features_voice, "steelblue",
  "PDP Residuals - Voice Analysis"
)
generate_pdp_plots(
  ars_fit_source, ars_baked_source, "ars_ads", features_voice, "steelblue",
  "PDP Source - Voice Analysis"
)

generate_pdp_plots(
  ars_fit_resid, ars_baked_resid, "ars_resid", features_liwc, "steelblue",
  "PDP Residuals - LIWC"
)
generate_pdp_plots(
  ars_fit_source, ars_baked_source, "ars_ads", features_liwc, "steelblue",
  "PDP Source - LIWC"
)

```


```{r lmer_tests}

# Test out LMER model using all variables from voice analysis and LIWC
ars_lmer <- lmer(
  ars_ads ~ avg_intonation_score + avg_speaking_rate + avg_loudness + avg_male_confidence +
    avg_estimated_age + neutrality_pct + happiness_pct + Clout_EN + Authentic_EN + Drives_EN + 
    Cognition_EN + Affect_EN + Social_EN + Lifestyle_EN + Physical_EN + Perception_EN + 
    (1 | session_id),
  data = ars_df %>% select(-session_in, -session_out)
)
summary(ars_lmer)

```


### ICORIA Finalize

```{r data_preparation}

# Prepare model for modeling - log transforming all features
log_variables = c(
  "neutrality_pct", "happiness_pct", "Authentic_EN", "Drives_EN", "Affect_EN",
  "Social_EN", "Lifestyle_EN", "Physical_EN", "Perception_EN"
)
icoria_df <- ars_modeling %>% 
  select(-ars_ads) %>% 
  mutate(across(all_of(log_variables), ~ log1p(.)))

# Get correlation between linguistic features and voice features with ARS residuals
cor_vars <- icoria_df %>% select(ars_resid, everything())
var_order <- c("ars_resid", setdiff(colnames(cor_vars), "ars_resid"))

cor_matrix <- cor(cor_vars, use = "complete.obs")
cor_melted <- melt(cor_matrix)

cor_melted$Var1 <- factor(cor_melted$Var1, levels = rev(var_order))
cor_melted$Var2 <- factor(cor_melted$Var2, levels = var_order)

ggplot(cor_melted, aes(x = Var2, y = Var1, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(value, 2)), size = 3) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#FF8A65",
    mid = "#F7F7F7",
    high = "#4A148C",
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1, 1)
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 5)),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 10)),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "grey50", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Feature Correlation Matrix",
    subtitle = "Linguistic, Vocal, and Physiological Features",
    fill = "Correlation"
  )

```


```{r model_prep}

# Create recipe for trainer
ars_recipe <- recipe(ars_resid ~ ., data = icoria_df) %>% 
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors(), one_hot = TRUE)

# Create model spec for both trainers
xgb_spec <- boost_tree(
  trees = tune(),
  tree_depth = tune(),
  min_n = tune(),
  learn_rate = tune(),
  loss_reduction = tune(),
  sample_size = tune()
) %>% 
  set_engine("xgboost") %>% 
  set_mode("regression")

xgb_params <- xgb_spec %>% 
  extract_parameter_set_dials() %>% 
  update(
    trees = trees(range = c(100, 1000)),
    tree_depth = tree_depth(range = c(3, 10)),
    min_n = min_n(range = c(2, 20)),            
    learn_rate = learn_rate(range = c(-3, -1)),
    loss_reduction = loss_reduction(range = c(-7, -2)),
    sample_size = sample_prop(range = c(0.5, 1.0))
  )

# Create the workflow used for both trainers
ars_workflow <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(ars_recipe) %>% add_model(xgb_spec)

# Create cross-validation fold for both trainers
set.seed(6297)
ars_cv <- vfold_cv(icoria_df, v = 10, strata = ars_resid)

```


```{r model_tuning}

# Tune models and save as cache
ars_tuned <- xgb_tuning(
  ars_workflow,
  ars_cv,
  xgb_params,
  here(model_folder, "ars_icoria.rds")
)

# Select best parameters and create workflow
ars_params <- select_best(ars_tuned, metric = "rmse")
ars_selected <- finalize_workflow(ars_workflow, ars_params)

# Fit the final models
xgb_ars <- fit(ars_selected, data = icoria_df)

```


```{r single_variable_plots}

# Extract variable importance out into a table
ars_importance <- xgb_ars %>% extract_fit_parsnip() %>% vi() %>% arrange(desc(Importance))

# Plot out the variable importance graphs
ars_importance %>%
  head(20) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Variable, Importance), y = Importance)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#4A148C", width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Variable Importance",
    subtitle = "Gradient Boosting Model — Top 20 Features by Gain",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Importance (Gain)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 5)),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 10)),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "grey50", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

# Extract the fitted model from workflows
ars_fit <- extract_fit_parsnip(xgb_ars)

# Prep training data through the recipe
ars_baked <- ars_recipe %>% prep() %>% bake(new_data = icoria_df)

# Phasic PDP and ARS PDP
features_voice <- c(
  "avg_speaking_rate", "neutrality_pct", "avg_intonation_score",
  "avg_loudness", "happiness_pct", "avg_estimated_age", "avg_male_confidence"
)
features_liwc <- c(
  "Perception_EN", "Physical_EN", "Affect_EN", "Social_EN",
  "Lifestyle_EN", "Authentic_EN", "Drives_EN", "Clout_EN", "Cognition_EN"
)
generate_pdp_plots(
  ars_fit,
  ars_baked,
  "ars_resid",
  features_voice,
  "#4A148C",
  subtitle = "Gradient Boosting Model — Effect of Voice Features on Residualized Interest Intensity"
)
generate_pdp_plots(
  ars_fit,
  ars_baked, 
  "ars_resid",
  features_liwc,
  "#4A148C",
  subtitle = "Gradient Boosting Model — Effect of Linguistic Features on Residualized Interest Intensity"
)

```


```{r interaction_summary}

# Extract interaction table
xgb_ars_engine <- extract_fit_engine(xgb_ars)
ars_prepped <- prep(ars_recipe) %>% bake(new_data = NULL) %>% select(-ars_resid) %>% as.matrix()
shap_ars <- shapviz(xgb_ars_engine, X_pred = ars_prepped)
shap_interact <- shapviz(xgb_ars_engine, X_pred = ars_prepped, interaction = TRUE)

# Create the pairwise interaction table
int_table <- get_interaction_table(shap_interact, ars_prepped, top_n = 30)

# Create cross-model interaction table
voice_vars <- c(
  "avg_speaking_rate", "avg_intonation_score", "avg_loudness",
  "avg_male_confidence", "avg_estimated_age", "neutrality_pct", 
  "happiness_pct"
)
liwc_vars <- c(
  "Clout_EN", "Authentic_EN", "Drives_EN", "Cognition_EN",
  "Affect_EN", "Social_EN", "Lifestyle_EN", "Physical_EN", 
  "Perception_EN"
)

cross_modal <- int_table %>%
  filter(
    (Var1 %in% voice_vars & Var2 %in% liwc_vars) |
    (Var1 %in% liwc_vars & Var2 %in% voice_vars)
  )

# Save the table into Word ready format
cross_modal_table <- cross_modal %>%
  head(20) %>%
  mutate(
    Rank = row_number(),
    value = formatC(value, format = "f", digits = 6)
  ) %>%
  select(Rank, `Voice Feature` = Var1, `Linguistic Feature` = Var2, `Interaction Strength` = value)

ft <- flextable(cross_modal_table) %>%
  set_caption("Top 20 SHAP Interaction Effects") %>%
  theme_booktabs() %>%
  align(align = "center", part = "all") %>%
  autofit()

save_as_docx(ft, path = here(results_folder, "cross_modal_interactions.docx"))

```


```{r shap_dependence}

# Neutrality Percentage and Physical
plot_pdp_interaction(
  ars_fit, ars_baked, "ars_resid",
  "neutrality_pct", "Physical_EN",
  title = "Neutrality Percentage × Physical",
  subtitle = "Cross-Modal Interaction Effect on Residualized Interest Intensity",
  resolution = 50
)

# Affect x Male Confidence
plot_pdp_interaction(
  ars_fit, ars_baked, "ars_resid",
  "avg_male_confidence", "Affect_EN",
  title = "Male Confidence Score × Affect",
  subtitle = "Cross-Modal Interaction Effect on Residualized Interest Intensity",
  resolution = 50
)

# Authenticity x Neutrality Percentage
plot_pdp_interaction(
  ars_fit, ars_baked, "ars_resid",
  "neutrality_pct", "Authentic_EN",
  title = "Neutrality Percentage × Authenticity",
  subtitle = "Cross-Modal Interaction Effect on Residualized Interest Intensity",
  resolution = 50
)

# Social x Average Speaking Rate
plot_pdp_interaction(
  ars_fit, ars_baked, "ars_resid",
  "avg_speaking_rate", "Social_EN",
  title = "Average Speaking Rate × Social",
  subtitle = "Cross-Modal Interaction Effect on Residualized Interest Intensity",
  resolution = 50
)

# Intonation x Affect
plot_pdp_interaction(
  ars_fit, ars_baked, "ars_resid",
  "avg_intonation_score", "Affect_EN",
  title = "Average Intonation Score × Affect",
  subtitle = "Cross-Modal Interaction Effect on Residualized Interest Intensity",
  resolution = 50
)

```


### ICORIA - Phasic Research

```{r control_model}

# Fit a control model to get residuals for phasic
phasic_random <- modeling_full_df %>%
  select(
    session_id, session_in, session_out, phasic_ads, segment_number,
    ad_position, program_format, age_average, females_pct
  ) %>%
  mutate(program_format = fct_relevel(program_format, "Sports", "Scripted", "Unscripted"))

control_phasic <- lmer(
  phasic_ads ~ segment_number + ad_position + program_format +
    age_average + females_pct + (1 | session_id),
  data = phasic_random
)
summary(control_phasic)

# Add the residuals back into the dataset
phasic_random <- phasic_random %>%
  mutate("phasic_resid" = residuals(control_phasic)) %>%
  select("session_id", "session_in", "session_out", "phasic_resid")

phasic_modeling <- phasic_df %>%
  left_join(phasic_random, by = c("session_id", "session_in", "session_out")) %>%
  select(-c("session_id", "session_in", "session_out"))

# Fixed effects table
re <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(control_phasic))
random_effects_note <- paste0(
  "Random Effects: Session intercept variance = ", 
  round(re$vcov[1], 6),
  ", Residual variance = ", 
  round(re$vcov[2], 6)
)

tbl <- tbl_regression(
  control_phasic,
  tidy_fun = broom.mixed::tidy,
  estimate_fun = ~ style_sigfig(., digits = 6),
  add_estimate_to_reference_rows = FALSE
) %>%
  add_glance_source_note() %>%
  modify_column_unhide(columns = statistic) %>%
  modify_header(statistic ~ "**t-value**") %>%
  modify_footnote(everything() ~ random_effects_note)

tbl %>%
  as_flex_table() %>%
  save_as_docx(path = here(results_folder, "lmer_phasic.docx"))

```


```{r log_transformation}

# Prepare model for modeling - log transforming all features
log_variables = c(
  "neutrality_pct", "happiness_pct", "Authentic_EN", "Drives_EN", "Affect_EN",
  "Social_EN", "Lifestyle_EN", "Physical_EN", "Perception_EN"
)
icoria_df <- phasic_modeling %>% 
  select(-phasic_ads) %>% 
  mutate(across(all_of(log_variables), ~ log1p(.)))

```


```{r descriptive_stats}

describe(phasic_modeling %>% select(-phasic_resid))

```


```{r correlation_plot}

# Get correlation between linguistic features and voice features with ARS residuals
cor_vars <- icoria_df %>% select(phasic_resid, everything())
var_order <- c("phasic_resid", setdiff(colnames(cor_vars), "phasic_resid"))

cor_matrix <- cor(cor_vars, use = "complete.obs")
cor_melted <- melt(cor_matrix)

cor_melted$Var1 <- factor(cor_melted$Var1, levels = rev(var_order))
cor_melted$Var2 <- factor(cor_melted$Var2, levels = var_order)

ggplot(cor_melted, aes(x = Var2, y = Var1, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(value, 2)), size = 3) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#FF8A65",
    mid = "#F7F7F7",
    high = "#4A148C",
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1, 1)
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 5)),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 10)),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "grey50", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Feature Correlation Matrix",
    subtitle = "Linguistic, Vocal, and Physiological Features",
    fill = "Correlation"
  )

```


```{r model_prep}

# Create recipe for trainer
phasic_recipe <- recipe(phasic_resid ~ ., data = icoria_df) %>% 
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors(), one_hot = TRUE)

# Create model spec for both trainers
xgb_spec <- boost_tree(
  trees = tune(),
  tree_depth = tune(),
  min_n = tune(),
  learn_rate = tune(),
  loss_reduction = tune(),
  sample_size = tune()
) %>% 
  set_engine("xgboost") %>% 
  set_mode("regression")

xgb_params <- xgb_spec %>% 
  extract_parameter_set_dials() %>% 
  update(
    trees = trees(range = c(100, 1000)),
    tree_depth = tree_depth(range = c(3, 10)),
    min_n = min_n(range = c(2, 20)),            
    learn_rate = learn_rate(range = c(-3, -1)),
    loss_reduction = loss_reduction(range = c(-7, -2)),
    sample_size = sample_prop(range = c(0.5, 1.0))
  )

# Create the workflow used for both trainers
phasic_workflow <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(phasic_recipe) %>% add_model(xgb_spec)

# Create cross-validation fold for both trainers
set.seed(6297)
phasic_cv <- vfold_cv(icoria_df, v = 10, strata = phasic_resid)

```


```{r model_tuning}

# Tune models and save as cache
phasic_tuned <- xgb_tuning(
  phasic_workflow,
  phasic_cv,
  xgb_params,
  here(model_folder, "phasic_icoria.rds")
)

# Select best parameters and create workflow
phasic_params <- select_best(phasic_tuned, metric = "rmse")
phasic_selected <- finalize_workflow(phasic_workflow, phasic_params)

# Fit the final models
xgb_phasic <- fit(phasic_selected, data = icoria_df)

```


```{r single_variable_plots}

# Extract variable importance out into a table
phasic_importance <- xgb_phasic %>% extract_fit_parsnip() %>% vi() %>% arrange(desc(Importance))

# Plot out the variable importance graphs
phasic_importance %>%
  head(20) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Variable, Importance), y = Importance)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#4A148C", width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Variable Importance",
    subtitle = "Gradient Boosting Model — Top 20 Features by Gain",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Importance (Gain)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 5)),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 10)),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "grey50", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

# Extract the fitted model from workflows
phasic_fit <- extract_fit_parsnip(xgb_phasic)

# Prep training data through the recipe
phasic_baked <- phasic_recipe %>% prep() %>% bake(new_data = icoria_df)

# Phasic PDP and ARS PDP
features_voice <- c(
  "avg_speaking_rate", "neutrality_pct", "avg_intonation_score",
  "avg_loudness", "happiness_pct", "avg_estimated_age", "avg_male_confidence"
)
features_liwc <- c(
  "Perception_EN", "Physical_EN", "Affect_EN", "Social_EN",
  "Lifestyle_EN", "Authentic_EN", "Drives_EN", "Clout_EN", "Cognition_EN"
)
generate_pdp_plots(
  phasic_fit,
  phasic_baked,
  "phasic_resid",
  features_voice,
  "#4A148C",
  subtitle = "Effect of Voice Features on Residualized EDA"
)
generate_pdp_plots(
  phasic_fit,
  phasic_baked, 
  "phasic_resid",
  features_liwc,
  "#4A148C",
  subtitle = "Effect of Linguistic Features on Residualized EDA"
)

```


```{r interaction_summary}

# Extract interaction table
xgb_phasic_engine <- extract_fit_engine(xgb_phasic)
phasic_prepped <- prep(phasic_recipe) %>% bake(new_data = NULL) %>% select(-phasic_resid) %>% as.matrix()
shap_phasic <- shapviz(xgb_phasic_engine, X_pred = phasic_prepped)
shap_interact <- shapviz(xgb_phasic_engine, X_pred = phasic_prepped, interaction = TRUE)

# Create the pairwise interaction table
int_table <- get_interaction_table(shap_interact, phasic_prepped, top_n = 30)

# Create cross-model interaction table
voice_vars <- c(
  "avg_speaking_rate", "avg_intonation_score", "avg_loudness",
  "avg_male_confidence", "avg_estimated_age", "neutrality_pct", 
  "happiness_pct"
)
liwc_vars <- c(
  "Clout_EN", "Authentic_EN", "Drives_EN", "Cognition_EN",
  "Affect_EN", "Social_EN", "Lifestyle_EN", "Physical_EN", 
  "Perception_EN"
)

cross_modal <- int_table %>%
  filter(
    (Var1 %in% voice_vars & Var2 %in% liwc_vars) |
    (Var1 %in% liwc_vars & Var2 %in% voice_vars)
  )

# Save the table into Word ready format
cross_modal_table <- cross_modal %>%
  head(20) %>%
  mutate(
    Rank = row_number(),
    value = formatC(value, format = "f", digits = 6)
  ) %>%
  select(Rank, `Voice Feature` = Var1, `Linguistic Feature` = Var2, `Interaction Strength` = value)

ft <- flextable(cross_modal_table) %>%
  set_caption("Top 20 SHAP Interaction Effects") %>%
  theme_booktabs() %>%
  align(align = "center", part = "all") %>%
  autofit()

save_as_docx(ft, path = here(results_folder, "cross_modal_interactions_phasic.docx"))
print(cross_modal)

```


```{r shap_dependence}

plot_interaction_lines(
  phasic_fit, phasic_baked, "phasic_resid",
  "avg_speaking_rate", "Lifestyle_EN",
  title = "Average Speaking Rate × Lifestyle"
)

plot_interaction_lines(
  phasic_fit, phasic_baked, "phasic_resid",
  "avg_estimated_age", "Lifestyle_EN",
  title = "Average Estimated Age × Lifestyle"
)

plot_interaction_lines(
  phasic_fit, phasic_baked, "phasic_resid",
  "neutrality_pct", "Clout_EN",
  title = "Neutrality Percentage × Clout"
)

plot_interaction_lines(
  phasic_fit, phasic_baked, "phasic_resid",
  "avg_intonation_score", "Lifestyle_EN",
  title = "Average Intonation Score × Lifestyle"
)

plot_interaction_lines(
  phasic_fit, phasic_baked, "phasic_resid",
  "neutrality_pct", "Perception_EN",
  title = "Neutrality Percentage × Perception"
)

```


```{r happiness_neutrality}

# doc <- read_docx()

# happiness_pct × all LIWC features
# doc <- doc %>% body_add_par("Happiness % × LIWC Features", style = "heading 1")

for (feat in features_liwc) {
  # tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
  # png(tmp, width = 1200, height = 800, res = 150)
  print(plot_interaction_lines(
    phasic_fit, phasic_baked, "phasic_resid",
    "happiness_pct", feat,
    title = paste("Happiness % ×", feat)
  ))
  # dev.off()
  # 
  # doc <- doc %>%
  #   body_add_par(paste("Happiness % ×", feat), style = "heading 2") %>%
  #   body_add_img(tmp, width = 6, height = 4)
}

# # neutrality_pct × all LIWC features
# doc <- doc %>% body_add_break() %>%
#   body_add_par("Neutrality % × LIWC Features", style = "heading 1")
# 
for (feat in features_liwc) {
  # tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
  # png(tmp, width = 1200, height = 800, res = 150)
  print(plot_interaction_lines(
    phasic_fit, phasic_baked, "phasic_resid",
    "neutrality_pct", feat,
    title = paste("Neutrality % ×", feat)
  ))
  # dev.off()
  # 
  # doc <- doc %>%
  #   body_add_par(paste("Neutrality % ×", feat), style = "heading 2") %>%
  #   body_add_img(tmp, width = 6, height = 4)
}
# 
# print(doc, target = here(results_folder, "happiness_neutrality_interactions_phasic.docx"))

```


```{r other_interactions}

already_plotted_voice <- c("happiness_pct", "neutrality_pct")

# Filter cross_modal for remaining pairs
remaining <- cross_modal %>%
  mutate(
    voice = ifelse(Var1 %in% voice_vars, Var1, Var2),
    liwc  = ifelse(Var1 %in% liwc_vars, Var1, Var2)
  ) %>%
  filter(!voice %in% already_plotted_voice)

# Build the doc
# doc <- read_docx()
# doc <- doc %>% body_add_par("Remaining Cross-Modal Interactions (Phasic)", style = "heading 1")

for (i in seq_len(nrow(remaining))) {
  v <- remaining$voice[i]
  l <- remaining$liwc[i]
  print(
    plot_interaction_lines(
      phasic_fit, phasic_baked, "phasic_resid",
      v, l,
      title = paste(v, "×", l)
    )
  )
  
  # tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
  # png(tmp, width = 1200, height = 800, res = 150)
  # print(plot_interaction_lines(
  #   phasic_fit, phasic_baked, "phasic_resid",
  #   v, l,
  #   title = paste(v, "×", l)
  # ))
  # dev.off()
  # 
  # doc <- doc %>%
  #   body_add_par(paste(v, "×", l), style = "heading 2") %>%
  #   body_add_img(tmp, width = 6, height = 4)
}

# print(doc, target = here(results_folder, "cross_modal_interactions_phasic.docx"))

```
